principle: Use generation providers for pixels and footage, Remotion for exact layout, Playwright for real product capture, and ffmpeg for final delivery.

routes.reason: Still frames are cheaper and clearer than generating motion before visual direction is approved.
Use Grok Imagine for fast text/image-to-video experiments and social cuts; finish typography in Remotion.
Use Veo or Sora for high-fidelity cinematic clips with audio; use Luma for exploratory variants.
Use Sora when OpenAI provenance, prompt workflow, or org access is the preferred lane.
Use Runway-style video editing when transforming existing footage; use Remotion for deterministic overlays.
Product demos should capture the real app first, then package with Remotion and ffmpeg.
Exact type, UI cards, captions, and data-driven scenes belong in code, not raw video generation.
Final encoding, compression, GIF conversion, and poster extraction should be deterministic.

preferredIntegrationOrder: Vercel AI Gateway for Grok/Veo when the site already runs on Vercel
Provider APIs for Sora, Veo, xAI, Luma, Runway, and Higgsfield when advanced controls are needed
Local MCP wrapper after the provider adapter layer is stable
Manual provider UI only for early creative exploration or unavailable APIs
